deduplication.title: Prevent Duplicate Alerts
deduplication.subtitle: Avoid sending the same alert multiple times by grouping similar alerts together.
deduplication.fingerprintFieldsHint: Select fields that make alerts similar (e.g., hostname, service). Leave empty to auto-detect based on your query.
deduplication.fingerprintFieldsTooltip: Choose which fields should match for alerts to be considered duplicates. For example, selecting 'hostname' means alerts from the same host will be grouped together. Different alert types (e.g., High CPU vs High Memory) are never grouped, even if they come from the same host. Leave empty for automatic detection based on your query type.
deduplication.timeWindowHint: Time period to group similar alerts. Leave empty to match your check interval.
deduplication.timeWindowTooltip: How long to remember recent alerts for grouping. If a similar alert happens within this time, it won't be sent again. Leave empty to use your alert's check interval.
deduplication.maxGroupSizeTooltip: Maximum number of deduplicated alerts to group together before sending a notification. Prevents overwhelming notifications when many similar alerts fire simultaneously.
deduplication.sendStrategyTooltip:
How to send grouped alerts:
• First with count: Send first alert with total count
• Summary: Send aggregated summary
• All: Send all alerts in one notification
deduplication.groupWaitTimeTooltip: Time to wait and collect similar alerts before sending a grouped notification. Allows batching of alerts that occur in quick succession.
deduplication.fingerprintFieldsHelp: Leave empty to auto-detect based on query (SQL: GROUP BY columns, PromQL: labels, Custom: condition fields)

alertSettings.cooldownTooltip: If the alert triggers then how long should it wait before sending another notification. e.g. if the alert triggers at 4:00 PM and the silence notification is set to 10 minutes then it will not send another notification until 4:10 PM even if the alert is still after 1 minute. This is to avoid spamming the user with notifications.
alertSettings.thresholdTooltip: Defines when the alert should trigger based on the event count or aggregated value. Example: If set to "> 100", the alert triggers when the count exceeds 100 events.
alertSettings.periodTooltip: Period for which the query should run. e.g. 10 minutes means that whenever the query will run it will use the last 10 minutes of data.
alertSettings.frequencyTooltipMinutes: How often the task should be executed. e.g. every 5 minutes.
alertSettings.frequencyTooltipCron: Standard UNIX cron format with 5 fields separated by space: [Minute 0-59] [Hour 0-23] [Day of Month 1-31] [Month 1-12 or JAN-DEC] [Day of Week 0-7 or '1L-7L', 0 and 7 for Sunday].
alertSettings.timezoneWarning: Warning: The displayed timezone is approximate. Verify and select the correct timezone manually.
alertSettings.destinationsTooltip: Select one or more destinations to send alert notifications.
alertSettings.templateTooltip: Optional: Select a template to use for all destinations in this alert. Templates allow you to customize the format of alert messages. If not selected, each destination will use its own configured template.
alertSettings.createsIncidentTooltip: If an alert can create an incident, it does not send a dedicated notification. Instead, a notification for the incident is sent when a new incident is created or a new alert type joins an existing incident.
alertSettings.helpLegendSample: a sample — the real value is set when the alert fires
alertSettings.helpLegendOpaque: added automatically when the alert fires
alertSettings.helpTemplateExplain: A template is the notification message that gets sent — the text plus variables like {'{'}alert_name{'}'}. Picking one here overrides each destination's own template, for this alert only.
alertSettings.helpCurrentNoOverride: No override set — each destination uses its own template:
alertSettings.helpCurrentNoDestinations: No destinations selected yet. Each destination will use its own configured template.
alertSettings.helpVariablesExplain: Variables are your own values you can drop into a notification message. Give each one a name and a value here, then write {'{'}your_name{'}'} anywhere in the template — it gets filled in when the alert fires.
alertSettings.helpBuiltInIntro: OpenObserve fills these in automatically — click one to copy it.
alertSettings.helpBuiltInFooter: Your stream's fields work as variables too. Advanced: {'{'}rows:N{'}'} limits how many rows show; {'{'}var:N{'}'} shortens a value.
alertSettings.helpYourVariablesEmpty: You haven't added any variables yet.
alertSettings.helpRowTemplateExplain: An alert can match many rows. The row template decides how a single row looks. Then {'{'}rows{'}'} in your main template drops in every matched row, already formatted.
alertSettings.helpRowTemplateTypeExplain: String produces plain text per row. JSON produces a JSON object per row, suitable for structured destinations.
alertSettings.helpWhyIntro: Variables let one template adapt to every alert. Instead of writing the value into the template, you reference it — and each alert fills in its own.
alertSettings.helpWhyWithoutDesc: You hard-code the value, so you need a separate template for every team or severity.
alertSettings.helpWhyWithDesc: One shared template references the variable; each alert sets its own value here.
alertSettings.helpVariablesExampleCaption: Add severity and team as variables, then use them anywhere in the template:
alertSettings.helpTemplateWhenDesc: Leave this empty and each destination uses its own template. Set an override when you want this one alert to send the same, consistent message to every destination — without editing each destination's template.
alertSettings.helpRowTemplateWhyDesc: An alert can match many records. The row template formats one record; {'{'}rows{'}'} then drops every formatted record into the main template — so you list all matches without writing a loop.
alertSettings.helpRowTemplateExampleCaption: Each matching row is formatted by the row template, then {'{'}rows{'}'} drops them all into the main template:
alertSettings.helpRowTemplatePreviewEmpty: No row template yet — type one on the Additional Settings tab to preview it here.
alertSettings.helpPreviewSelectEmpty: Pick a template above to preview the message it would send.
alertSettings.helpCurrentBodyEmpty: This template has no body to preview.

reports.cronFormatTooltip:
Pattern: * * * * * * means every second.
Format: [Second (optional) 0-59] [Minute 0-59] [Hour 0-23] [Day of Month 1-31, 'L'] [Month 1-12] [Day of Week 0-7 or '1L-7L', 0 and 7 for Sunday].
Use '*' to represent any value, 'L' for the last day/weekday.
Example: 0 0 12 * * ? - Triggers at 12:00 PM daily.

promptVariableGuide.title: Available Prompt Variables
promptVariableGuide.description: These variables can be used in the prompt above. How each one is filled in depends on what the Eval Job evaluates.

inputOutput.span: Map these to the span fields holding the input and output.
inputOutput.trace: Input and output from the trace's root span.
inputOutput.session: Not available — a session has no single input or output.

statistics.span: Map this name like any other scorer variable when evaluating a single span.
statistics.trace: Trace metrics: span count, duration, LLM and tool calls, errors, tokens, and cost.
statistics.session: Session metrics: trace and turn counts, duration, and errors.

spans.span: Map this name like any other scorer variable when evaluating a single span.
spans.trace: A filtered subset of the trace's spans, chosen by the Eval Job's Span Selector.

steps.span: Map this name like any other scorer variable when evaluating a single span.
steps.trace: Spans in time order; the middle is folded when the trace is very long.
steps.session: Traces in time order, represented as conversation turns.

promptVariableGuide.indexDescription: {spansVar} and {stepsVar} arrive as JSON arrays. Reference the whole variable — indexed access like {indexedVar} is not supported.
